content.text: Secrets are genuinely gone and the existing cluster is a wrong/leftover deploy (7.17.9, no TLS, no security). I'll start clean: tear it down, generate TLS + password secrets, and deploy a correct 8.11.1 secured cluster. First, generate certs.

input.command:
cd /tmp && rm -rf escerts && mkdir escerts && cd escerts
cat > san.cnf <<'EOF'
[req]
distinguished_name = dn
x509_extensions = v3
prompt = no
[dn]
CN = elasticsearch
[v3]
subjectAltName = @alt
basicConstraints = CA:FALSE
keyUsage = digitalSignature,keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth,clientAuth
[alt]
DNS.1 = es-http
DNS.2 = es-http.elasticsearch
DNS.3 = es-http.elasticsearch.svc
DNS.4 = es-http.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
DNS.5 = es-cluster
DNS.6 = *.es-cluster
DNS.7 = *.es-cluster.elasticsearch
DNS.8 = *.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc
DNS.9 = localhost
IP.1 = 127.0.0.1
EOF
# CA
openssl genrsa -out ca.key 2048 2>/dev/null
openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key ca.key -sha256 -days 3650 -subj "/CN=es-ca" -out ca.crt 2>/dev/null
# server cert
openssl genrsa -out tls.key 2048 2>/dev/null
openssl req -new -key tls.key -out tls.csr -config san.cnf 2>/dev/null
openssl x509 -req -in tls.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial -out tls.crt -days 3650 -sha256 -extensions v3 -extfile san.cnf 2>/dev/null
ls -la && echo "OK" && openssl x509 -in tls.crt -noout -text | grep -A12 "Subject Alternative"

kubectl -n elasticsearch create secret generic es-http-tls --from-file=tls.crt=tls.crt --from-file=tls.key=tls.key --from-file=ca.crt=ca.crt
echo "=== secrets ==="
kubectl -n elasticsearch get secret

content.text: Now write the full manifest: ConfigMap, headless Service, es-http Service, StatefulSet, PDB.

input.content:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${POD_NAME}
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - es-cluster-0
      - es-cluster-1
      - es-cluster-2
    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - es-cluster-0.es-cluster
      - es-cluster-1.es-cluster
      - es-cluster-2.es-cluster
    node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]
    node.store.allow_mmap: false
    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl:
      enabled: true
      key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
      certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
      certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl:
      enabled: true
      verification_mode: certificate
      key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
      certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
      certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-cluster
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: es-cluster
  ports:
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
      targetPort: 9300
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  selector:
    app: es-cluster
  ports:
    - name: https
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-cluster
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: es-cluster
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es-cluster
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-cluster
  replicas: 3
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: es-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: es-cluster
    spec:
      securityContext:
        fsGroup: 1000
      initContainers:
        - name: fix-perms
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /usr/share/elasticsearch/data"]
          securityContext:
            runAsUser: 0
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          env:
            - name: POD_NAME
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.name
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 9200
            - name: transport
              containerPort: 9300
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - sh
                - -c
                - "curl -s -k -u elastic:$ELASTIC_PASSWORD https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -qE '\"status\":\"(green|yellow)\"'"
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 30
            timeoutSeconds: 5
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "250m"
              memory: "1Gi"
            limits:
              cpu: "1"
              memory: "1536Mi"
          volumeMounts:
            - name: es-config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
              readOnly: true
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
      volumes:
        - name: es-config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
            items:
              - key: elasticsearch.yml
                path: elasticsearch.yml
        - name: certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 5Gi

content.text: Classic bootstrap deadlock: the headless Service doesn't publish DNS for not-ready pods, so discovery can't resolve peers. Fix with `publishNotReadyAddresses: true`.

kubectl -n elasticsearch patch svc es-cluster -p '{"spec":{"publishNotReadyAddresses":true}}'
# restart pods so they re-resolve
kubectl -n elasticsearch delete pod -l app=es-cluster
sleep 8
kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods

content.text:
Done. The 3-node Elasticsearch cluster is deployed and verified.

## Summary

The observed state was stale — the namespace actually contained a **wrong leftover deploy** (image `7.17.9`, no TLS, no security) and the `es-http-tls` / `elastic-password` secrets were **missing**. I started clean.

**What I did:**
1. **Generated TLS material** — a self-signed CA + server cert with SANs for `es-http`, `*.es-cluster`, `localhost`, etc.
2. **Tore down** the wrong StatefulSet, Services, ConfigMap, and PVCs.
